name: Eggos + Peanut Butter
notes: Add chocolate chips if you want a treat. Toast them a little longer for crispy edges.

name: Bag Salad Kit + Kevin's Chicken
notes: Throw in extra spinach, cherry tomatoes, shredded cheese — whatever's around. The kit dressing is usually enough.

name: Frozen Veggie Meal
notes: Annie's, Trader Joe's, whatever looks good. Grab 4–5 varieties so you have options.

name: Scrambled Eggs + Toast
notes: Season however you feel — everything bagel seasoning, hot sauce, fresh herbs if you have them.
recipe: Crack 2–3 eggs into a bowl, scramble with a fork.
Heat a small pan on medium-low, add a bit of butter.
Pour in eggs. Don't touch them for 30 seconds.
Gently push the eggs around until just barely set — they'll keep cooking off heat.
Toast your bread while the eggs rest. Season however you like.

name: Veggie Quesadilla
notes: Great way to use up random veggies. Frozen corn, canned black beans, cheese — whatever's around.
recipe: Heat a pan on medium. No oil needed if you're using a nonstick.
Lay a tortilla flat, add shredded cheese on one half.
Add whatever veggies: frozen corn (thawed), canned black beans, spinach, peppers.
Fold the tortilla over.
Cook 2–3 min per side until golden and melty.
Cut in half. Season with hot sauce, sour cream, salsa — whatever you have.

name: Greek Yogurt Bowl
notes: Add granola, fruit, honey, or nut butter. Whatever sounds good.

name: Pasta + Jarred Sauce
notes: Throw in frozen spinach or canned white beans for extra substance. Season the sauce with garlic powder, red pepper flakes, parmesan.
recipe: Boil salted water in a pot. Add pasta.
Cook until just tender (check the box — usually 8–10 min).
While it cooks, warm the jarred sauce in a second pan.
Optional: stir in frozen spinach or canned white beans.
Drain pasta, add to sauce. Toss.
Season with salt, red pepper flakes, parmesan if you have it.

name: Salsa Verde Chicken Enchiladas
notes: Pre-cooked shredded chicken makes this stupidly easy. White cheese melts better than yellow. Don't skimp on the sour cream.
recipe: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Spread a thin layer of salsa verde on the bottom of a baking dish.
Fill each tortilla: a scoop of shredded chicken, a spoonful of sour cream, a handful of cheese. Roll up, seam-side down.
Pour the rest of the salsa verde over the top. Cover with more cheese.
Bake 20–25 min until bubbly and the cheese is starting to brown.
Serve with extra sour cream.

shopping_items.item: White cheese (Monterey Jack or queso fresco)

name: Ground Beef Tacos
notes: Keep toppings simple — shredded cheese, sour cream, salsa. Corn tortillas hold up better if you warm them in the pan first.
recipe: Brown ground beef in a pan over medium-high heat, breaking it up as it cooks.
Drain most of the fat. Season with cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, salt — go by smell and taste.
Add a splash of water, stir, let it simmer a minute.
Warm tortillas in the same pan or a dry skillet.
Load them up: beef, cheese, sour cream, salsa, whatever else you have.

name: Air Fryer Nachos
notes: Use the air fryer basket lined with foil for easy cleanup. Canned jalapeños, black beans, whatever you have.
recipe: Line air fryer basket with foil.
Spread chips in a single-ish layer.
Add toppings: canned beans, cheese, jalapeños.
Air fry at 350°F for 3–5 min until the cheese is melted.
Top with sour cream, salsa, avocado — whatever's around.

name: Teriyaki Ground Beef Rice Bowl
notes: Bottled teriyaki sauce is completely fine. Add a fried egg on top if you have one.
recipe: Cook rice (microwave pouches are great here).
Brown ground beef in a pan, breaking it up.
Drain most of the fat. Pour in a few glugs of teriyaki sauce, stir to coat.
Let it simmer a minute until sticky.
Serve over rice. Add whatever toppings — frozen edamame, shredded carrots, a fried egg.

name: Air Fryer Gyoza / Dumplings
notes: Frozen gyoza straight from the bag into the air fryer. Dipping sauce: soy sauce + a little rice vinegar + chili flakes.
recipe: Spray air fryer basket lightly with oil.
Arrange frozen gyoza in a single layer.
Air fry at 375°F for 8–10 min, flipping halfway.
Mix dipping sauce: soy sauce + rice vinegar + chili flakes, or use store-bought.

name: Ramen + Soft Egg + Veggies
notes: Instant ramen is the base — the egg and veg make it an actual meal. Baby spinach wilts right in.
recipe: Boil water, cook ramen noodles.
While noodles cook, soft-boil an egg (6–7 min in boiling water) or just crack one straight into the broth.
Add seasoning packet (or miso paste + soy sauce instead).
Throw in a handful of spinach or frozen edamame at the end.
Top with the egg, a drizzle of sesame oil if you have it.

name: Air Fryer Tofu + Peanut Sauce Bowl
notes: Press the tofu first — the drier it is, the crispier it gets. Bottled peanut sauce is great; or thin peanut butter with soy sauce and lime.
recipe: Press tofu for at least 15 min (tofu press or wrap in a towel with something heavy on top).
Cut into cubes, toss with a little soy sauce and cornstarch.
Air fry at 400°F for 12–15 min, shaking halfway, until crispy.
Cook rice while the tofu is going.
Toss any veggies (broccoli, edamame, shredded carrots) in the air fryer for the last 5 min.
Assemble: rice, tofu, veggies, peanut sauce drizzled over everything.

name: Buddha Bowl
notes: Grain + protein + veg + sauce. No rules. Use whatever's in the fridge. Tahini or miso ginger dressing both work great.

shopping_items.item: Microwave grain pouches (rice, quinoa, or farro)

name: Shakshuka
notes: Fancy-sounding, stupidly easy. Canned crushed tomatoes do all the work. Feta on top is not optional. Pita for dipping.
recipe: Heat a pan on medium, add olive oil.
Add diced onion and peppers if you have them — or skip straight to the tomatoes.
Pour in a can of crushed tomatoes. Season with cumin, smoked paprika, salt, garlic powder.
Let it bubble for 5 minutes, then make little wells with a spoon.
Crack an egg into each well. Cover the pan.
Cook 5–8 min until whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Top with crumbled feta, fresh herbs if you have them. Serve with pita.

name: Greek Salad + Hummus + Pita
notes: Cucumber, olives, cherry tomatoes, feta, red onion if you have it. Olive oil + lemon + oregano is all the dressing you need.

name: Ground Beef + Couscous + Herbs
notes: Couscous is just hot water and 5 minutes — barely cooking. Lemon and cumin make ground beef taste very different from taco night.
recipe: Pour boiling water over couscous (equal parts), cover with a plate, wait 5 min. Fluff with a fork.
Brown ground beef in a pan. Drain fat.
Season with cumin, cinnamon (just a pinch), garlic powder, salt, lemon zest if you have it.
Serve beef over couscous. Squeeze lemon over everything.
Top with fresh parsley, crumbled feta, or whatever sounds good.

name: Air Fryer Falafel + Pita
notes: Frozen falafel — stuff into pita with hummus, cucumber, and whatever you've got.
recipe: Air fry frozen falafel at 375°F for 10–12 min, flipping halfway.
Warm pita in the air fryer last 2 min.
Load up: falafel, hummus, cucumber, tomato, a drizzle of hot sauce or tzatziki.

name: White Bean Tomato Soup (Crockpot)
notes: Dump everything in, walk away for 4–6 hours. Mash some beans against the side to thicken it. Crusty bread is essential.
recipe: Dump into the crockpot: 2 cans white beans (drained), 1 can crushed tomatoes, 1 can diced tomatoes, broth, garlic powder, Italian seasoning, salt, red pepper flakes.
Optional: add a parmesan rind if you have one — incredible flavor.
Cook on low 4–6 hours or high 2–3 hours.
Mash some beans against the side to thicken it up.
Stir in a handful of spinach at the end. Serve with bread.

name: Avocado Toast
notes: Everything bagel seasoning is the move. Add a fried egg or sliced cherry tomatoes if you want more substance.

name: Cottage Cheese Bowl
notes: Go savory: cucumber, cherry tomatoes, everything bagel seasoning, olive oil. Or sweet: honey, berries, granola. Both are great.
